plainEnglish: This is the stricter Level AAA version of 2.4.11: when an element receives keyboard focus, no part of it may be hidden by author content. The entire focused element must be visible, not just partly.
whyItMatters: Keyboard users can't fully see the focused control—and may miss part of its state or label—if any portion is covered by sticky or floating content.
quickCheck: Tab through the page and confirm the focused element is fully visible, with no part covered by sticky or overlapping content.
commonMistakes: Sticky elements that partially overlap the focused control (allowed at AA, but not at AAA).
Insufficient scroll-margin, so the top or bottom edge of the focused element is clipped.
Floating chat or help widgets that cover part of the focused control.

codeExamples.label: Focused element is fully clear of overlays
Overlay clips part of the focused element
